summary: Deprecated audit trail for the AppEvents sound-scheme bundle. The current app clears many HKCU\AppEvents\Schemes sound-event values, but the publishable control surface is tracked separately from this implementation bundle.

setting.name: System Sound Assignments
setting.casual_explanation: Windows maps many system events to sound files. This tweak clears those mappings so common Windows events stop making sound.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable system sounds when they want a quieter desktop, fewer interruptions, or a cleaner workstation environment.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Clearing system sounds can make Windows quieter, but it also removes audible feedback for notifications, prompts, and system events.
setting.professional_notes: The app clears a large set of HKCU\AppEvents\Schemes sound-event values by writing empty strings, and the repo provenance marks the tweak as repo-backed. This pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source that publishes the exact AppEvents registry-write bundle as the supported control surface for managing system sound assignments.

allowed_values.label: Primary Microsoft mapping not captured
allowed_values.meaning: This research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source that publishes the AppEvents bundle as a supported registry control for disabling system sounds.

allowed_values.label: Observed app silent-scheme profile
allowed_values.meaning: The current app writes empty strings to many AppEvents sound mappings so the associated Windows events no longer play sounds.

targets.notes: Tracked as an observed multi-value AppEvents bundle only.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app clears many AppEvents sound mappings, but this pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source for the exact AppEvents bundle contract.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles where this dataset has not validated the AppEvents bundle contract

states.rationale: Do not publish the AppEvents write bundle as validated until a primary Microsoft source is captured.

windows_defaults.notes: This keeps the sound-scheme tweak documented without overstating confidence in the registry contract.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Keep the AppEvents write bundle blocked until a primary Microsoft source is captured.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to document the current tweak without publishing it as a validated Windows control.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a fully sourced silent sound-scheme preset.
tradeoffs: The AppEvents mapping remains blocked from validated publication.

evidence.title: Repo source note for audio.disable-system-sounds
evidence.summary: The repo source note links the AppEvents sound-scheme bundle to upstream nohuto research and marks the tweak as repo-backed.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes empty strings to many HKCU\AppEvents\Schemes sound mappings.

decision.why: The repo-backed implementation is clear, but this pass did not capture a primary Microsoft source for the exact AppEvents bundle contract.
blocking_issues: No primary Microsoft source for the exact HKCU\AppEvents\Schemes bundle written by the app was captured in this pass.
